vulnerability.title: Verify Permissions on cron.d
vulnerability.description: To properly set the permissions of /etc/cron.d, run the command: $ sudo chmod 0700 /etc/cron.d
vulnerability.recommendation: Remediation script: # Remediation is applicable only in certain platforms if rpm --quiet -q kernel-core; then find -H /etc/cron.d/ -maxdepth 0 -perm /u+s,g+xwrs,o+xwrt -type d -exec chmod u-s,g-xwrs,o-xwrt {} \; else >&2 echo 'Remediation is not applicable, nothing was done' fi

triage_decision.reason: Changing permissions on /etc/cron.d to 0700 is a standard security hardening measure that does not affect system functionality, access, or critical services, and aligns with the policy to classify file permission fixes as safe_to_remediate.

previous_review_verdicts.feedback: The proposed plan correctly addresses the vulnerability by setting /etc/cron.d to 0700 permissions with existence check and verification. concerns: Does not verify that /etc/cron.d is not a symlink (though symlink to directory is likely acceptable)

No explicit error handling for chmod failure

Consider ensuring the target is a regular directory and not a symlink if that is a concern
Log the changes for audit purposes
